title: Handicap Index: your golfing passport
microFact: Your Handicap Index is the average of your BEST 8 of your last 20 rounds — it measures your potential, not your average.
lesson:
The WORLD HANDICAP SYSTEM (WHS, since 2020) gives every golfer one portable number: the HANDICAP INDEX, calculated to one decimal (e.g., 28.4).

How it works:
• Every round you post becomes a SCORE DIFFERENTIAL — roughly, how you played relative to the course's difficulty (using its rating and slope, byte 37).
• Your Index = the average of your BEST 8 differentials from your last 20 rounds.

Best-8-of-20 means the Index tracks your demonstrated POTENTIAL — what you shoot on good days — not your typical day. That's why most golfers only beat their handicap about 1 round in 4, and why that's normal, not failure.

Getting started needs just 54 HOLES posted (any mix of 9s and 18s), and your initial Index arrives. It then updates overnight after every posted round. Maximum Index is 54.0.

This number is your entry ticket: tournaments, club guest rounds abroad, even conversations begin with "what do you play off?" Your goal: make that answer real, then make it shrink.

visual.caption: Handicap Index = average of your best 8 differentials from the last 20 rounds.

quiz.question: Your Handicap Index is built from which scores?
options: Your worst 10 of 20, to be safe
Average of all your rounds this year
Your best 8 of your last 20 differentials
quiz.explanation: Best 8 of the most recent 20 — so the Index reflects your potential. Most golfers play OVER their handicap most days.
